sentence_reviews.text: A model learns a data distribution or transformation process capable of producing new samples.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: GAN arXiv abstract describes a generative model capturing the data distribution and generation of samples (lines 41-44); DDPM arXiv abstract describes diffusion probabilistic models and image synthesis (lines 41-44); Flow Matching arXiv abstract describes probability paths transforming noise and data samples and sample generation (lines 41-45).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected generative-model sources directly support distribution/process learning and new sample generation.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Generative modeling is to connect Probability theory, Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs to downstream capabilities including Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy, Autoregressive / next-step prediction, Reconstruction objectives.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: VAE arXiv abstract discusses probabilistic models, variational lower bounds, and latent variables (lines 37-40); GAN arXiv abstract discusses data distributions (lines 41-44); DDPM arXiv abstract discusses diffusion probabilistic models and variational bounds (lines 41-44); Flow Matching arXiv abstract discusses probability paths and likelihood/sample quality (lines 41-45).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support probabilistic generative modeling and objectives, but not the exact downstream list, especially cross-entropy/autoregressive/reconstruction nodes as phrased.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: VAE arXiv abstract discusses variational lower-bound optimization (lines 37-40); Flow Matching arXiv abstract reports likelihood and sample quality (lines 41-45).
sentence_reviews.notes: Likelihood-based evaluation/training is supported; cross-entropy is not directly named in inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: DDPM arXiv abstract says its lossy decompression scheme can be interpreted as a generalization of autoregressive decoding (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected source mentions autoregressive decoding only by analogy/generalization, not as a direct downstream substrate.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: VAE arXiv abstract discusses approximate inference and lower-bound optimization in latent-variable probabilistic models (lines 37-40); DDPM arXiv abstract discusses lossy decompression and variational bounds (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: Reconstruction/decompression objectives are consistent with the inspected sources, though the exact node relationship is synthesized.

sentence_reviews.text: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: VAE arXiv abstract on modeling/inference conditions (lines 37-40); GAN arXiv abstract on data-distribution capture and training process (lines 41-44); Flow Matching arXiv abstract on probability-path design (lines 41-45).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support probabilistic/modeling assumptions but not the exact deployment-objective limitation.

sentence_reviews.text: Generative modeling uses Probability theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: VAE arXiv abstract on directed probabilistic models (lines 37-40); GAN arXiv abstract on data distribution and sample probability (lines 41-44); DDPM arXiv abstract on diffusion probabilistic models (lines 41-44); Flow Matching arXiv abstract on probability paths (lines 41-45).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected sources directly support probability as central to generative modeling.

sentence_reviews.text: Generative modeling uses Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: GAN arXiv abstract on capturing the data distribution (lines 41-44); Flow Matching arXiv abstract on Gaussian probability paths and transforming between noise and data samples (lines 41-45).
sentence_reviews.notes: Distributions/probability paths are directly supported; uncertainty/beliefs are broader but within the atlas dependency label.

sentence_reviews.text: Generative modeling uses Latent variables & hidden state as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: VAE arXiv abstract discusses continuous latent variables per datapoint (lines 37-40); DDPM arXiv abstract calls diffusion probabilistic models a class of latent variable models (lines 41-44).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected sources directly support latent variables in important generative-model families.

sentence_reviews.text: Generative modeling uses Optimization theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: VAE arXiv abstract discusses optimizing a variational lower-bound estimator with stochastic gradient methods (lines 37-40); GAN arXiv abstract discusses adversarial training (lines 41-44); Flow Matching arXiv abstract discusses training CNFs by regression (lines 41-45).
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected sources directly support optimization/training objectives as common ingredients.

edge_reviews.notes: Probabilistic models, distributions, and probability paths directly support a core probability dependency.
Data distributions and probability paths directly support the dependency; core strength is defensible.
Latent variables are directly supported for VAEs and diffusion models; common strength is appropriate because not every generative model is latent-variable based.
Training objectives and optimization are directly supported in the inspected generative-model sources; common strength is appropriate.
